questions.question: What is the stabilizer of the state |+⟩ = (|0⟩ + |1⟩)/√2?
questions.explanation: X|+⟩ = |+⟩, so X stabilizes |+⟩. The stabilizer group is ⟨X⟩ = {I, X}. Note that Z|+⟩ = |-⟩ ≠ |+⟩, so Z does not stabilize |+⟩.

questions.question: How many independent generators are needed for an n-qubit stabilizer state?
questions.explanation: An n-qubit stabilizer state requires exactly n independent generators. The full stabilizer group has 2ⁿ elements, but they can all be generated by products of n generators.

questions.question: What are the stabilizers of the Bell state |Φ⁺⟩ = (|00⟩+|11⟩)/√2?
questions.explanation: The Bell state has two stabilizer generators: X₁X₂ (both qubits flip together in |Φ⁺⟩) and Z₁Z₂ (both qubits have same parity in computational basis).

questions.question: Why must all elements of a stabilizer group commute?

options.text: Non-commuting operators would give contradictory eigenvalue constraints
It's required by the Pauli group structure

questions.explanation: If S₁|ψ⟩ = |ψ⟩ and S₂|ψ⟩ = |ψ⟩, then S₁S₂|ψ⟩ = |ψ⟩. For Paulis, if S₁ and S₂ anticommute, then S₁S₂ = -S₂S₁, leading to a contradiction (the eigenvalue would be both +1 and -1).

questions.question: In the tableau representation, how many bits are needed to represent an n-qubit stabilizer state?
questions.explanation: The tableau uses n generators, each requiring 2n bits (n for X, n for Z components) plus a phase bit. Total: O(n²) bits. This is exponentially more efficient than storing the 2ⁿ amplitudes.

questions.question: What is the stabilizer generator for vertex v in a graph state?

options.text: Xᵥ times Z on all neighbors of v

questions.explanation: For graph state on graph G, the stabilizer for vertex v is Kᵥ = Xᵥ ∏_{u∈N(v)} Zᵤ, where N(v) is the neighborhood of v. This reflects the CZ gates connecting v to its neighbors.

questions.question: Every stabilizer state is locally equivalent (via single-qubit unitaries) to a graph state.
questions.explanation: True. This is a fundamental theorem about stabilizer states. Any stabilizer state can be transformed into a graph state by applying appropriate single-qubit Clifford gates to each qubit.
